title: Boat Sandpit with Sail
summary: A boat-shaped sandbox built from 2x12 hull sides with a real cloth sail on a mast — angled hull ends are the only tricky cut.
description:
This is a sandbox shaped like a boat: two long 2x12 sides mitered at 45° on each end so they meet at a point, deck boards across the stern, angled hull pieces closing the bow, and a mast with a sewn sail rigged with real rope through grommet-style stitched corners. The hull geometry is simpler than it looks — it's the same 45° angle repeated at both ends of both sides, so once you've cut one angle correctly you're just repeating it three more times.

Use an exterior-rated glue and stainless or coated deck screws throughout; this sits outside, filled with damp sand, for years. The sail isn't just decorative — sewing a channel into the fabric and threading the dowel through it, then lashing the whole thing to the mast with exterior rope, is what turns a plain sandbox into a boat kids actually play pretend in.
timeLabel: 4–6 hrs, plus sewing time for the sail

materials.name: 2x12 board, 8 ft
materials.note: The hull sides — this is what gives the sandbox its boat shape.

materials.name: 2x4 board, 12 ft
materials.note: The mast pieces come from this longer board.

steps.title: Cut the hull side angles
steps.body: Mark and cut 45° angles at both ends of both 2x12 hull sides, using a speed square as your angle guide.

steps.title: Attach the deck boards
steps.body: Position the two hull sides on a flat, level surface. Apply exterior glue and screw the deck boards to the edges of the hull sides with 2-1/2" deck screws.

steps.title: Close the bow and stern
steps.body: Flip the assembly over. Apply glue to the mating surfaces and screw the 45"-long 2x4 and 2x6 hull end pieces to the angled ends of the hull sides.

steps.title: Install the mast
steps.body: Screw the two mast pieces to the inside of the hull sides, centered, with glue and at least four screws per side. Drill a hole in the top of the assembled mast for the sail boom dowel and thread the dowel through.

steps.title: Sew and rig the sail
steps.body: Cut the fabric to 45" wide by 3 yards long. Fold and hem the sides and ends, then fold the corners in and stitch to create a channel for rope at each corner. Screw the I-bolts into the corners of the deck boards, then tie rope between the I-bolts and the sail, pulling the sail over the boom dowel and tightening it down.

images.alt: Boat-shaped wooden sandbox with a real cloth sail and mast
